prompt: A RAG assistant returns a confident answer that cites a real source document, but the cited document is the wrong one (US policy instead of EU policy). What does this most strongly indicate?
choices: A retrieval failure: the wrong chunk was surfaced, and generation faithfully used what it was given
A generation failure: the model ignored the provided context
The embedding model needs to be retrained on new data
The temperature setting is too high

prompt: Which techniques specifically improve the RETRIEVAL stage (rather than the generation prompt)? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Hybrid search combining semantic and keyword matching
Metadata filtering to restrict candidates by region, date, or access level
Re-ranking a candidate set with a more precise model before sending the top few onward
Instructing the model to answer only from the provided context

prompt: Two chunks are nearly identical in meaning and differ only by a distinguishing token like a region code, and pure semantic search keeps retrieving the wrong one. Which combination best resolves this AND why?
choices: Add metadata filtering plus hybrid search, because filtering removes ineligible chunks and keyword matching weights the exact distinguishing term
Increase top-k to 50, because retrieving more chunks always raises accuracy
Lower the chunk overlap to zero, because overlap is what causes duplicate retrieval
Raise the model temperature, because more randomness helps it pick the right source
